exemplar.title: To what extent does a single thin layer of acrylic paint coating prevent the tarnishing of metal substrates (10cm2 of Zn(s) and Mg (s) and 15cm2 Fe(s)), after being submerged in 20cm2 of saltwater solution (5.99 mol/dmÂ³) for 72 hours, as measured by the comparison of the increase in conductivity (Î¼S/cm)?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does a single thin layer of acrylic paint coating prevent the tarnishing of metal substrates (10cm2 of Zn(s) and Mg (s) and 15cm2 Fe(s)), after being submerged in 20cm2 of saltwater solution (5.99 mol/dmÂ³) for 72 hours, as measured by the comparison of the increase in conductivity (Î¼S/cm)?

strengths: Data are presented in raw and processed tables with consistent headings, units and linkages to appendices
Graphs are multi-series and colour-coded, making trends easy to follow
Uncertainties for probe readings and mean values are calculated and reported
weaknesses: Apparatus uncertainties (time, volume, area) are not specified or propagated, and error bars are missing on graphs
Duplicated time points in tables create ambiguity
Percentage uncertainty calculations show inconsistencies between coated and uncoated samples
Photographs lack scale bars, reducing precision of visual comparison
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data is clear and largely precise (band 3â4), but significant omissions in uncertainty propagation and minor inaccuracies (duplicate data rows, incomplete uncertainty reporting) limit the data-processing quality to the middle of the 1â4 range, warranting 3 marks.

strengths: Research question clearly stated within a specific, realâworld context of jewellery corrosion
Comprehensive background linking electrochemical theory to practical corrosion issues
Well-justified control variables and rationale for trial repetition
Clear step-by-step methodology with diagrams allowing reproduction with minor clarifications
weaknesses: Chosen NaCl concentration (5.99 mol dmâ»Â³) lacks quantitative justification and conflicts with solubility data
Coating thickness control (âone brush dipâ) and drying conditions (temperature, humidity) are underspecified
Exact metal mass and alloy grade are not provided
Conductivity probe calibration steps are omitted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The research question is embedded in a very specific context (band 5), and methodological considerations and description are described in sufficient detail to allow reproduction with few ambiguities (band 4). However, key quantitative details (salt concentration rationale, probe calibration, coating and sample specifications) are missing, bringing the holistic score to the upper 3â4 band; hence 4 marks.

strengths: Conclusion links observed conductivity differences to corrosion theory and cites quantitative differences
Theoretical percentage-fit calculation to literature corrosion rates is well presented
weaknesses: Conclusion paragraph is incomplete and cuts off mid-sentence
Contradictory statements (e.g., âconductivity is unchangedâ) conflict with data trends
Discussion of magnesium results is inconsistent with processed data
No critical evaluation of literature source reliability or experimental limitations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion describes outcomes and engages with scientific context (band 3â4), but inconsistencies, omissions, and lack of critical source evaluation prevent full justification and consistency with all analysis, placing it solidly at 3 marks.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (probe calibration, coating thickness, trial duration) are identified and linked to potential data impacts
Relevant and realistic improvements are proposed for each identified weakness
Shows clear understanding of how proposed changes could enhance reliability
weaknesses: Does not rank weaknesses by their relative severity or likely effect on results
Improvements lack feasibility discussion or preliminary design considerations
No quantification of how each improvement would improve uncertainty or data quality
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation lists specific weaknesses and corresponding realistic improvements (band 3â4) but does not explain the relative impact or feasibility in depth, aligning with a mid-range score of 3 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated and immediately embedded in a clear, real-life context (tarnishing of jewellery in seawater).  Background information on each metal, the corrosion environment and previous studies is provided, demonstrating why the question is significant and appropriate.  The context is therefore specific rather than merely broad; however, a few details (e.g., quantitative justification for chosen NaCl concentration) are thin, so the strand does not merit the full 6 marks.

Processing includes mean calculations, change-in-conductivity, percentage uncertainty and theoretical-fit values.  Calculations appear internally consistent and trends are interpreted.  Nonetheless, some values (e.g., duplicated time points, use of âchangeâ when raw is cumulative) hint at inaccuracies, so the strand aligns with âsome significant omissions/inaccuraciesâ but still above the 1-2 band.

Raw tables, processed tables and multi-series graphs are presented with units, uncertainties and colour coding.  Headings are consistent and data are easy to follow, though some duplicate headings (two â0.5 hâ rows) reveal minor imprecision.  Overall communication is clear and largely precise, satisfying the 3-4 band.

For every weakness a realistic improvement is proposed (e.g., micrometer for paint thickness, extended time frame, more frequent early measurements).  Suggestions are relevant and sufficiently described, yet explanations of how each improvement would quantitatively enhance data quality are brief, so the strand sits comfortably in the 3-4 band rather than 5-6.

Absolute probe uncertainty and percentage uncertainties for each mean are provided, but propagation and uncertainty bars on graphs are missing, and apparatus uncertainties for time, volume and area are not considered.  Hence there is evidence of consideration, yet several significant omissions remain, placing the work at the lower end of the 3-4 band.

The student compares experimental rates to literature corrosion rates for each metal and computes theoretical % fit, demonstrating some engagement with wider scientific context.  However, sources are not critically evaluated and discrepancies (e.g., unrealistic NaCl molarity) are not deeply analysed, so the comparison is relevant but not fully justified.

Specific weaknesses are listed (probe calibration, coating thickness, short duration, sample stability).  Each is briefly explained, showing understanding of how they could influence results, but the relative impact (which was most serious) is not discussed in depth, fitting the 3-4 descriptor.

The student identifies relevant variables, gives a control table and justifies six trials per metal.  Key methodological features (coated vs. uncoated, identical solution volume, temperature, humidity) show thought toward obtaining sufficient and relevant data.  Nonetheless, explanations are uneven: saturation calculation for 5.99 mol dmâ»Â³ is chemically unrealistic, coating thickness control is only âsame brush dipped onceâ, and probe calibration is omitted.  Thus considerations are âdescribedâ rather than âfully explainedâ.

Step-by-step instructions, materials list and diagrams would allow another student to reproduce the work with limited ambiguity.  Remaining gaps include precise metal mass/grade, exact drying environment for paint, and how sand-paper cleaning is standardised.  Because some clarification would still be needed, reproduction is feasible but not âcompleteâ â fitting the 3-4 band ceiling.

The conclusion clearly states that acrylic paint reduces corrosion and links to observed conductivity differences; quantitative differences are cited and tied to oxidation theory.  Minor inconsistencies (e.g., magnesium discussion) prevent full alignment with all processed data, so the work is âdescribedâ and largely consistent rather than fully justified, corresponding to the upper end of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated within a specific context, identifying substrate areas, solution volume and concentration to frame the investigation effectively.
The background description successfully contextualizes tarnishing by linking user experience to electrochemical corrosion, showing relevance to real-world jewellery problems.
The background on saltwater corrosion does not justify why a 5.99 mol dmâ»Â³ concentration was chosen over typical seawater levels (~0.6 mol dmâ»Â³); provide rationale.
The oxidation equations for Zn, Mg and Fe are presented clearly with correct stoichiometry, helping to justify conductivity as a measure of corrosion.
The choice of 5.99 mol dmâ»Â³ NaCl lacks quantitative justification and is chemically unrealistic for saturation; clarify or revise this concentration selection.
The controlled variables table is comprehensive and clearly links each variable to its rationale and implementation.
Listing saltwater concentration as 5.99 mol dmâ»Â³ without reconciling it with PubChemâs solubility data may mislead; reconcile with literature or adjust.
The preparation of âsaturatedâ saltwater (7 g in 20 cmÂ³) is miscalculated; clarify massâvolume relationship and temperature dependency.
The oxidation list lacks a brief explanation of conditions (e.g., pH, Clâ» concentration) under which each reaction proceeds; add context to improve methodological clarity.
The justification for using conductivity measurements is persuasive, linking probe sensitivity to corrosion rates without disturbing visual changes.
The methodology omits the exact mass and alloy grade of Zn, Mg and Fe used; specifying these parameters is essential for reproducibility.
Drying of the acrylic coating is stated as 15 min without specifying temperature, humidity or airflow; define drying conditions for consistency.
The uncertainty calculation section presents formulas clearly but does not specify the absolute uncertainties of volume, time and area measurement.
Specify apparatus uncertainties and describe how they are combined when calculating percentage uncertainty to improve data-processing completeness.
Table 1 on page 14 has duplicated time points (0.5 h and 24 h), which creates ambiguity; correct or merge these rows.
Table 2âs percentage uncertainties use identical values for some coated and uncoated data, suggesting a check of calculations for inconsistencies.
Method step 6 omits calibration of the conductivity probe, risking systematic error; add calibration before and after each trial.
Include a calibration step in the methodology that details solutions and frequency to ensure probe accuracy is maintained.
The results section refers to appendices for raw data, demonstrating clear linkage between processed and background data.
Graphs lack error bars to represent propagated uncertainties, reducing the precision of data communication.
Add uncertainty bars on plots by propagating absolute and percentage uncertainties from the probe and timed measurements.
Conductivity change calculations are transparent but do not include uncertainty propagation, which is needed for full data-processing quality.
Photographs in Figures 5â7 effectively illustrate corrosion differences but lack scale bars or magnification details for precise visual comparison.
The theoretical % fit calculation is well presented, linking experiment to literature corrosion rates for Zn.
The conclusion paragraph is incomplete and cuts off mid-sentence; ensure it fully summarizes quantitative findings and theory.
The conclusion states âconductivity is unchanged,â which contradicts observed trends showing significant conductivity increases; revise for accuracy.
Compare discrepancies between experimental and literature corrosion rates by critically evaluating source reliability and experimental limitations.
The evaluation identifies weaknesses and improvements but does not explain the relative impact of each limitation on data quality.
Rank methodological weaknesses by severity or likely effect on corrosion rate measurements to deepen the evaluation.
Extension suggestions list alternate coatings and multi-layer tests but omit feasibility or preliminary design considerations.
